AD: Steep Pyrenean ridges, U-shaped valleys; glacial cirques; granite cols above timberline
AE: Low gulf shore with sabkha flats; interior gravel sheets and longitudinal dune ridges
AF: High Hindu Kush with snow-fed gorges; gravel fans and closed desert basins westward
AG: Volcanic limestone islands with interior ridges; fringing reef shelf on Atlantic swell
AL: Dinaric limestone ridges and polje basins; narrow coastal plain with steep offshore drop
AM: Volcanic plateau between fold belts; large basin ringed by treeless alpine steppe
AO: Atlantic escarpment to humid coast; broad interior plateau with miombo and inselbergs
AR: Andean wall east of wide pampas; dry Patagonian steppe, windworked shingle southward
AT: Eastern Alps and deep lake troughs; foreland hills roll north toward wide river lowlands
AU: Arid interior sandplain and dunes; monsoon savanna belts across the far northern tropics
AZ: Caspian lowshore, loess bluffs; inland semi-arid basins west of forested mountain folds
BA: Karst plateaus with sinking rivers; narrow corridors along faulted limestone gorges
BB: Low coral caps with aeolian limestone; windward Atlantic surf versus leeward lagoon flats
BD: Monsoon delta of braided silty channels; floods over mudflats and shifting river bars
BE: North Sea low polders and sand; Ardennes forest upland rises sharply in the southeast
BF: Sahel inselbergs on seasonal grass plains; thorn scrub and scattered gallery woodland
BG: Balkan mountains; embayment coast, forested foothills, and wide interior basins inland
BH: Flat limestone archipelago on a shelf; tidal flats link islets on calm gulfhead waters
BI: Scarps above rift lake chains; mist forest on wet eastern slopes above narrow littoral
BJ: Coastal lagoon bars; interior low plateaus on deeply weathered crystalline basement rock
BN: Tropical rainforest on a narrow northern fringe; inland sandstone hills on old basement
BO: Altiplano basins above four thousand meters; escarpment drops to humid lowland margin
BR: Humid lowland forest in the north; dissected shield uplands toward the Atlantic escarpment
BS: Low calcareous banks with tidal creeks; shoals shift on a shallow shelf between hurricanes
BT: Eastern Himalayan relief with glacier north faces; gorges through humid temperate forest
